title: PRACTICAL DETACHMENT | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: you
Krishna a warm welcome to all of you
for this Gita live session. Today's topic is practical detachment. All of us are attached
to people, to situations, to circumstances, to things and many of us have realized that
there is a linkage to our attachments.
And the misery which we encounter in our lives. In fact, Lord Buddha said the root cause of
all our misery is attachments. So therefore, in every religion, some talk, some understanding
about rules, regulations for detaching ourselves from things, from people, from situations.
So today we are going to discuss this in detail based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada,
teachings of Lord Shri Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita. So before we dive into this discussion,
let's start with prayers seeking the blessings of Prabhupada, Lord Shri Krishna, Lord Chaitanya
Prabhu, so that we can understand this topic.
Not only understand, we can get some insights, how we can practically apply this in our lives
and not remain too attached to things, to people, to situations, to circumstances.
So please fold your hands, if possible, and join. Let's chant prayers together.
Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale

So, the first principle, the first aspect about practical detachment which we should
all thoroughly and clearly understand is what is real detachment?
Before we even pursue, at least we should have clarity, what is real detachment?
What is real renunciation?
What the scriptures are trying, are telling us to pursue?
Many people have an understanding, detachment means you should not be wearing good clothes,
you should not be living in a good, comfortable house.
A person who is renounced is supposed to be in some forest or in some mountains.
Very, very , matted hairs, tattered clothes, generally we have about a renounced
or a detached person.
But there is some lacuna, some mistake in this understanding.
We have examples of kings who were also called kings.
Raj Rishis, Raja Rishis, Raja Rishis means they were kings, they were living in palaces
amidst opulence, married, with lot of so-called guards, lot of comforts, lot of luxuries around
them but they were simultaneously called Rishis, Rishis at heart.
Example, Parikshit Maharaj, he was the emperor of the whole world, grandson of Arjuna, son
of Abhimanyu.
He was emperor of the whole world but when he was cursed that he will leave the body
or die in seven days, although he was living in a palatial comforts, but he renounced everything
in moments notice.
And went to the bank of the river.
And went to the bank of the river.
And for next seven days heard the Shrima Bhagavatam.
This is an example how a person may be living in a palatial house but deep within in his
heart, he is not attached.
It's there so he is so-called enjoying that comfort but knowing very well that this is
not mine.
The example is given just like coconut.
When it ripens that shell, within that the coconut gets detached, separated out.
So today we will be discussing about how to pursue, how to achieve this real detachment
and not get deluded or bewildered with false renunciation.
And this true detachment is very much possible even while we are living amidst family, doing
our worldly duties, executing the responsibilities, it is necessary to be detached.
And that is what the Lord is asking us to strive for in the Bhagavad Gita.
He gives an example of lotus, although it's living, although it is growing in a dirty
pond,
but it is
detached from the surroundings, it's untouched by the dirt around it.
So I'll share with you an example to illustrate what is real detachment.
Let's say a person is walking down the road and he sees a wallet, purse fallen on the
road, lying on the road.
And you can also see these 2000 rupee notes coming out from that purse and it's quite
thick, it's quite tempting and alluring even for a person who is of high integrity.
Broadly speaking, a person can take one of the three steps or three options.
One of the three steps or three options.
One of the three steps or three options.
The first option is the person sees left, right, up, down, ensuring that nobody is watching,
he can pick that wallet and walk away.
That's option one.
The second option is a person after seeing the wallet, there is a momentary temptation,
allurement.
Money is sweeter than gold.
Money is sweeter than gold.
Money is sweeter than honey.
And right there it's available and nobody is watching.
But then a person has good sense, he has values.
He says it's not mine, leaves it and walks away.
It's not mine.
That's option number two.
Although there are many combinations of these three options, broadly speaking, there are
three options.
The first option is the person gets allured, yes, quite valuable, 2000 rupee notes, bundle
of them, lying on the road.
He understands using his intellect, discerns that it is not mine, nirmama, but he also
understands that there is something.
There is something.
There is someone who has lost this wallet.
It belongs to someone.
One understanding, it doesn't belong to me.
It's better than thinking that, okay, I can take it and I can enjoy.
But the best understanding is, the best option is where a person understands that it does
not belong to me, but it belongs to someone else.
And strives to find.
Find to whom it belongs and goes and gives it back.
I'm sure all of you will agree, option three is the best option.
It is not mine, but it belongs to someone and it has not just come from nowhere.
Someone has lost that and I'm sure he must be quite miserable having lost that wallet.
He picks it up and tries to give it, tries to identify to whom it belongs and give it
back.
So the first option, the people in, who try to enjoy all the facilities, all the comforts,
thinking that it belongs to me.
I deserve it.
I have worked hard.
It's mine.
Aham ameti.
I have worked hard.
I have built this bungalow.
I have bought this car.
It belongs to me.
And I have right to enjoy.
That mentality is called a karmi.
Karmi means who works hard and wants to enjoy the fruits of the result, thinking that this
results belong to me.
It's because of me.
I have worked hard.
It's because of my ability.
So that's called karmi.
Okay.
Okay.
And there are some gyanis who understand that this is not mine, nirmama, it's not mine,
but I have nothing to do with it.
Just like the option number two, just walks away, leaving it aside.
I am not the body, Brahma Satya Jagat Mithya, all this Jagat Mithya, it's false, I have
nothing to do with it.
Okay.
It's the second option.
There are spiritualists who pursue this understanding that this Jagat is Mithya.
Money is material.
It's Maya.
I have nothing to do with money and hence renounce.
I have nothing to do with comforts, this luxuries.
I just renounce.
I am detached from it and to practice the detachment, they stay away from it, they shun
it.
Okay.
So, this is the third option.
Option number three, we may be blessed with certain abilities, certain skills.
We may be blessed with comfortable house, nice family members.
We realize that I have been blessed with these luxuries, these possessions.
It does not belong to me.
Okay.
It belongs to the Lord, the true proprietor, Bhokta Ram Yagna Tapasam, Bhokta, he is the
real enjoyer.
I am just a Shetragna, a knower of the field of activities.
I am that Atma.
I am not this body, all the skills, the abilities which I have, which this body, I am able to
speak well, I am able to sing well, I am able to see clearly, these all are abilities given
to me.
Because of those abilities, I am able to interact in this world, because of some intelligence,
I am able to fetch a good job.
Knowing that I have been blessed with these abilities, it is not mine, it is gifted to
me and the results of my activities also truly belong to the person who has been blessed
with these abilities.
Because of certain abilities, somebody is earning a lakh of rupees, because of lack
of some abilities, somebody is earning 5000 rupees.
So a person understands that these abilities, this body, these skills, the intelligence,
the mind, analytical faculties are coming from the Lord.
Okay.
And hence offers the result of all his activities for the pleasure and satisfaction of the Lord.
Knowing that it truly belongs to him.
He offers and for his, for those accepts as Prasadam, leads a regulated lifestyle, without
being attached.
So the real detachment of consciousness, that awareness, nirmama, it is not mine.
Therefore our scripture says prematurely without cultivating that consciousness, that understanding,
which comes gradually by practicing a process, nirmama, with right understanding, cultivating
the right knowledge.
If prematurely one gives up his family, goes to the forest, that renunciation will not last
because the foundation for that renunciation is false.
Prabhupada says a person may even go to a forest but can get attached to the tree under
which he is practicing.
He can get attached.
This is my tree.
This is my dwelling.
He can get attached to the Kamandaloo.
This is my vast land.
He can get attached to the Kamandaloo.
He can get attached to the Kamandaloo.
the begging bowl, it is my bowl. A person in contrast can be living in a palatial house,
have a nice beautiful altar with an understanding that this house belongs to the Lord. I am
just a caretaker. I am a servant of the Lord and my duty is to please the Lord, to serve
the Lord. I go out to work so that I can earn money and serve the Lord nicely. The
Lord is the center of my activities, the center of my house. I saw this practical example
I mentioned in our previous session also. We had gone to somebody's house, a very devoted
lady, very Krishna conscious person and in the hospital where they operate, where they
function, one full floor is dedicated to the Lord.
They call it Mother's Temple of the Lord and in the house also as you enter one room
the Lord, the Archa Vigraha, the deity of the Lord is in that room. It is a very good
example of how we can make the Lord the center of our activities. And when one leaves with
that consciousness that is called Nirmama and that is real detachment, and that is what
we should strive for.
The second term is a place where we have to have those different guardrails, our true
point is having understood what is real detachment, the second principle is also related to the
first one and that is what is false detachment or false renunciation. Although we have understood
what is real renunciation or real detachment, let us also clearly understand what is false
renunciation. Because we see various permutations and combinations of renunciations, detachment in
the world around us and we can be bewildered if we don't have a clear understanding. That's why
in today's time we see lot of God men flourishing, doing wonderful as far as their so-called
acquisitions are concerned, very rich, very opportunistic.
People give lot of Dakshina. Why? Because you know they seem to be very renounced. Big beard,
shaved heads, Bagwe, saffron clothes and people are but because they don't have a
clear understanding of what is real renunciation, they often get cheated.
So,
I will share with you four kinds of false renunciation.
The first one is although there are many other types also but four of them which I'm sure you
all can relate with. The first one is called Markat Vairagya. Markat means monkey. This
reminds me of a story. There were group of monkeys who decided that they will fast the
whole day. Somehow they got a good sense of it that let us fast. So, all in a group they came
together and decided we will fast tomorrow. Then, one of them went and told this group of
monkeys, tomorrow when we will be fasting who knows we will be very tired, very weak, at least let us go and sit near the tree with the
fruits are here. At least we may not have the strength to climb the tree, so let's
sit near the tree. And they all went and sat near the tree, laden with fruits. And
then one of them suggested, what you have said is so correct, so true. What if
tomorrow when we are fasting, we may not even have the strength
What about climbing the tree? It also requires lot of strength, lot of energy.
So all of them decided they all climbed the tree and sat, so that they can fast
tomorrow, and because they will not have enough energy, they can
make arrangements in advance.
Another one,
Another one suggested that tomorrow when we are fasting, you know, we may not have energy
to pluck the fruits from the tree.
Why don't we pluck it and keep it along with us?
We may not eat it, but we can keep it along with us.
Who knows, we may not have the energy to even pluck the fruits from the tree.
So they all plucked the fruits and kept it along with us then.
And then another wise monkey said, we have plucked the fruits, why not, what if we don't
have energy even to chew the fruits tomorrow, why not we just chew the fruits and keep it
in the mouth.
We may not ingest it, we may not take it in, but we can chew it and keep it in case.
In case, the time the fast gets over, we can take it in.
And they were deliberating, the day passed by and before they could even fast, they had
already taken the fruits and the fast was broken.
So many times Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita says the mind, if it is uncontrolled, it's
a worst enemy.
Mind will give reasons.
Even if we have some indigestion.
Even if we have some inclination to follow some rules and regulations, let's say some
Ekadashi is there, today's Ekadashi, mind will give reasons and will make us break that
fast, break that resolve, break that resolution.
So therefore, Yogi should strenuously try to control his sense, strenuously, with determination,
we can easily guide him to success, to be guided.
Just like sometimes, we have trucks going up the hill, a lot of energy is required.
And by chance, if the truck is quite old, quite delapidated and it breaks down in between,
it is quite possible it can glide back, because it has to go against the slope.
To go down the slope it is quite easy, lot of energy is consumed.
So, likewise a Yogi will be spices for the Deva deva.
Otherwise even for his inner soul, lotus water produced from the one of the Himalayas,
puj distri Wahブ Chan . He does He Grav aerospace for more stair value in lower tiens, walkんな
He has to strive to control his senses, to control his mind.
So the first false renunciation is Markat Vairagya where we give lot of freedom to the
whims of the mind, the suggestions of the mind and we fall prey to them.
The second kind of renunciation or detachment is Broken Caught Renunciation.
This is a nice name on some incidences, anecdotes just to make us easy to understand.
What is Broken Caught Renunciation?
There was a person who had a nice khat, khat means a cot, in Hindi it is called khat.
He got a good nice cot in dowry and over the years it became quite old and a time came
when one of the legs of the cot broke.
And he tried his best to put the leg together, tie it out with some ropes.
Could not do it, because the cot was in very bad shape and he realised that since the cot
cannot be repaired.
and he didn't have any money to buy a new cot, he started sleeping on the floor.
So he observed that he is sleeping on the floor, they asked him, why are you sleeping
on the floor?
So he started philosophizing and preaching to them, don't you know sleeping on cot,
we should give up all these comforts and luxuries.
Human life is temporary.
Human life is meant for detachment.
He started preaching to them.
Why should we be attached to the cot?
Life is meant to cultivate detachment and renunciation.
So he started preaching and philosophizing, showing off his detachment to his friends.
I am not attached, but the root cause of detachment is not real detachment.
It is because he does not, he cannot afford a cot.
So a person may be living in an impoverished condition, may be very detached, but quite
possible, it is circumstantial.
It is not coming from within, it's not coming out of real understanding.
It is out of some compulsion, some circumstantial circumstance.
And that renunciation is called broken understanding.
And that renunciation is called broken understanding.
You might have many sadhus in holy places begging, not necessarily because of their
church, but because they had no other option, but to beg and to live.
Some of them, not everyone.
The third kind of renunciation, false renunciation, I request devotees, I will be asking all of
you. The first one we talked about Markat Vairagya. The second one is called Broken
Court Renunciation. I request all of you to write down later on when I complete this part
of the discussion. The third one, False Renunciation or False Detachment is called Shamshaan Vairagya.
Sometimes we all have experienced some near and dear one or other place we have gone to
crematorium for someone, the cremation ceremony. So you would have seen you know on the walls
Ram Naam Satya Hai and people also chant this Ram Naam Satya Hai and when you go to the
crematorium for a moment you feel quite blessed.
You feel detached. You feel the futility, the uselessness of pursuing material things
which will go in no time. The person who is lying dead, whole life he struggled, worked
so hard, he had so much of stress, went through so much of anxiety, now he is lying there.
It's all become null and void. All his struggles, all his pains, all his anxieties, all his
anxieties, he struggled for money, he struggled for loans, he struggled for so many things,
just like that. It's all become null and void. He had so much of property, so much of assets,
it's all left behind. So seeing that a person can experience futility, what is the use? They
feel that yes Ram Naam Satya Hai but that renunciation, that detachment, that detachment,
does not last. A person, so long as he is in the crematorium, he experiences that detachment.
Once he comes out home, again all that detachment surfaces up, manifest in the life. It is very
short lived and that renunciation, that Vairagya is called, Shamshaan Vairagya. This is another
kind of renunciation which we might explain.
We might experience out of some guilt. We do something wrong. I remember when I was
in hostel. So generally you share rooms so you have friends coming from different cities,
different backgrounds. So in the second year I was sharing the room with couple of students.
They had started in
the second year smoking. Generally in the first year there is some bit of regulation,
some bit of control. But by the time many of them come to the second year, smoking and
drinking becomes quite common. So one of them had started smoking. He started off, he was
there for a few days, maybe just in some parties or friends are forcing him, why not try one.
So he starts on it and then around 7 o'clock in the evening or something Fehler will happen.
He starts smoking again. He will come when he does not like it. So he will eat everyone.
So he has a practice. So heplнес him, he sags and that cannot do. When anyone should believe that this
will come, there will come to reformatism – or droitatism. He will do that whole thing, and at the end he will not do anything because he will stop and mask himself.
So he started off and he didn't realize that very soon he started becoming addicted.
It didn't dawn on him, he didn't realize and there was a time when he was smoking about
10 to 12 cigarettes a day.
He was my roommate and because there was, in hostel you have always limited money coming
in from parents a little cautious, he is all alone, he can get spoiled.
So by 15th, by 20th of every month you are broke, all the money what you have received
is all over.
And this friend of mine won't believe towards the end of the month he would not have money
to even buy cigarettes and he even resorted to smoking BDs.
BD is a, some of you may be aware, it is the cheaper version of cigarette.
And every day I have seen him many, many times, every time he would smoke there was an element
of guilt feeling.
And I have seen him n number of times, at least 20 number of times saying that this
is the last cigarette of my life.
Very emphatically.
Very emphatically.
Very forcefully out of that guilt feeling, out of that feeling of getting trapped, that
feeling that I am getting addicted, out of frustration, why am I getting into this habit.
I started off as a social kind of fad but I am getting hooked out of that guilt feeling.
Many times I have seen him raising his hand, this is the last cigarette of my life.
And you won't believe I met him about few years back again.
And he is still smoking.
So that renunciation, the detachment which comes out of guilt because you have done something
wrong, you realize that it is wrong.
But later on you cannot resist it because there is force from within.

The desires, the lusty desires, it propels us, goads us, forces us to act in sinful ways, much against our wish.
Prabhupada said, the monkeys, they are living in the forest.
They seem to be very detached.
They are living on trees, they are not living in well-built houses with comforts.
They are not dressed.
We have sometimes Naga Babas, Kumbh Mela, very detached, ash smeared on the body, not wearing any clothes.
It's quite visibly that they are detached, not even having clothes.
So monkeys are not having clothes.
They are living on trees.
So,
all the symptoms of a renounced person are there in the monkeys.
They are living on trees.
They have no fixed residence.
They are not having proper clothes, naked.
They also
eat fruits and berries, whatever is available in the forest.
Generally, when a person who is renounced is meditating in the forest,
he doesn't have a provision where somebody is
supplying him cooked food, whatever is available in the forest, the fruits, he just manages with it.
So the monkeys also are managing with fruits and nuts, whatever is available in the forest.
So we see all the symptoms of a detached and renounced person are there in that monkey.
But what is his consciousness?
What is he seeing?
He is always chasing, thinking about the monkey.
He is always chasing, thinking about the monkey.
He is always engaging, indulging, in sense gratification, unrestrictedly.
Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita says,
One who is renounced externally, but else in his mind, in his sense object, he is called a pretender.
Externally he is renounced.
So what really matters is not externals.
To have an external,
shaved head, beard,
wearing these clothes, it just takes two minutes.
Many times we have dramas, we have plays, so people can dress like Narada Muni, you can have costumes.
It doesn't take much time to change that dress, but to become Narada Muni, in conscious,
it's not easy. To dress like Narada Muni is very easy.
You get dressed, you can rent a dress,
hundred,
perfectly like Narada Muni.

what's your willing.
A person may be living amidst opulence.
Many times Prabhupada was mistaken that the people would come,
He would also ask people for donations so that he can build beautiful temples glorifying the Lord.
So one time a person asked Swamiji, don't you think money is Maya?
Because generally, you know, sadhus, they just shun money.
Money is Maya.
So Prabhupada says, we don't shun money.
We want to collect millions of dollars in the day.
And by the end of the day, before we sleep, we should have spent it for the service of the Lord.
Money is not Maya.
Utilizing that money, thinking that it is mine and meant for my sense gratification, my enjoyment, that's the cause of bondage.
That is Maya.
But knowing that this money belongs to the Lord and money can be engaged, can be used to serve the Lord nicely, that money is not Maya.
Knife is not.
Good or bad depends on how we use it.
The knife in the hand of a terrorist can kill a person.
The same knife in the hand of a surgeon can save a life.
So money is not Maya.
Living in a house is not Maya.
Having good relations with family members is not Maya.
If we have the Lord in the center, the Lord in our consciousness,
the satisfaction and pleasure,
the gratitude of the Lord, gratitude that this all things are given to me by the Lord, belongs to the Lord and have to be meant, have to be used for the service of the Lord, that is real detachment.
And that has to be cultivated.
Just like there is something called as law of farm.
You cannot go to a farm and with a seed in your hand and says, okay, here is the seed I am putting in the ground.
Tomorrow, the fruits.

There is a law of farm, you have to put the seed, the seed will sprout, you have to put manure, you have to put water, you have to protect that sapling, slowly it grows, it becomes a young tender plant, and then it grows, then it has branches, offshoots, becomes a tree, and then it flowers, and then one day there is a fruit, and the fruit ripens and you can enjoy.
It happens over a period of time.
You have to cultivate it.
And that is why this law is called the law of farm.
So, this law is also applicable.
We have to cultivate attachment to the Lord.
We have to cultivate detachment, which automatically happens when we cultivate attachment to the Lord.
And that is what our scriptures are recommending.
Human life is, and that attachment to the Lord and detachment from matter can happen irrespective of what circumstances we are in.

Even a person living amidst family, amidst opulence, busy with his household duties can also cultivate detachment, can also cultivate attachment to the Lord, and that is recommended rather than artificially, prematurely.
You know, even in birth, generally in the womb it takes nine months for the fetus to fully develop.
And sometimes, you know, there are premature deliveries, six months, seven months, they call it premature delivery.
So, likewise, even detachment, it takes
It takes time.
It's a process.
We have to follow the process very strenuously.
Sincerely, and then this detachment will come.

Before we get into those five things, there is another verse which says, tushhti pushti
shudh apaya.
When we eat, when we eat some nice wonderful foodstuff and if we are hungry, then there
is tushhti.
Tushhti means there is an experience of happiness.
Nice, if you are eating some nice food, moment you put that morsel in the mouth, you experience
joy, you experience happiness, tushhti.
You don't have to speculate, you don't have to intellectualize, you don't have to…
Generally, when you put nice palatable food in your mouth, you experience joy, tushhti,
you feel good, you feel pleasing sensations.
Then pushti.
You experience satisfaction of hunger, nourishment, strength.
You don't require some certificate from someone.
When you start eating, you experience joy, you experience that you are getting energized,

When we eat prasadam, it is not only nourishment for the body, physical body, it's also nourishment
